heading: Definitions
text:
21 U.S.C. § 802. Definitions.

As used in this subchapter:

(1) The term "administer" refers to the direct application of a controlled substance to the body of a patient or research subject by—
(A) a practitioner (or, in his presence, by his authorized agent), or
(B) the patient or research subject at the direction and in the presence of the practitioner.

(6) The term "controlled substance" means a drug or other substance, or immediate precursor, included in schedule I, II, III, IV, or V of part B of this subchapter. The term does not include distilled spirits, wine, malt beverages, or tobacco, as those terms are defined or used in subtitle E of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

(16) The term "narcotic drug" means any of the following whether produced directly or indirectly by extraction from substances of vegetable origin, or independently by means of chemical synthesis, or by a combination of extraction and chemical synthesis:
(A) Opium, opiates, derivatives of opium and opiates...

(32) The term "schedule I", "schedule II", "schedule III", "schedule IV", and "schedule V" refer to the schedules established by section 812 of this title (21 U.S.C. § 812).

For state law equivalents see N.Y. Public Health Law § 3302 (definitions) and N.Y. Public Health Law § 3306 (schedules of controlled substances).
